setup_friction: Wizard field examples list health/nursing/finance/controls but never says 'software' or 'software engineering' is a valid field — a SWE has to guess to use software_engineering to keep tech boards on.
Location is asked as a free city string with no explanation that it becomes a HARD gate with a 30-mi metro-variant radius; 'Austin' vs 'Austin, TX' expand differently.
Nothing warns that a broad senior/lead exclusion list will silently cut ~half the raw results at the hard-gate.

create_project scaffolds config.json + a stub experience.md + output/, but does NOT scaffold preferences.{json,md} — only the wizard's apply() writes those, so a programmatic setup must write them via workspace.preferences_paths(slug).
CareerOneStop (the Guide's stated #1 local-coverage lever) has no key in .env and no onboarding step nudges the user to add it; it silently does nothing.

seeding.notes: Drove the exact +Add Companies pipeline: ats_detect.parse_line -> probe_count -> company_registry.save_companies, tagged software_engineering. All 15 parsed; probe found 8 live (Cloudflare 226, Palantir 276, Notion 151, Ramp 126, Homeward 5, Indeed/Netflix/Square 0), 5 unreachable (bad slug/tenant guesses: CrowdStrike, Bumble, SailPoint, Gusto, Tesla), 2 direct/junk (deliberate). KEY FINDING: save_companies adds ALL parsed entries regardless of probe result — Validate is advisory, not a gate — so 5 unreachable + 2 junk boards were still written. An AI is bad at guessing ATS slugs (I got 5/13 real-company board slugs wrong); parse succeeds but the slug is a coin-flip. Junk failed at scrape time with soft NameResolutionError lines (working as designed). Seeds barely paid off: only Ramp+Homeward reached the top 40, both sub-optimal (over-remote).

errors: careeronestop skipped — credentials missing (known unkeyed gap)
jooble skipped — JOOBLE_API_KEY unset
careerjet skipped — CAREERJET_AFFID unset
Brave company discovery skipped — BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY unset
Adzuna rate-limit back-off x2 (~60s each; no hard 429)
2 junk seed URLs logged soft [direct] fetch error -- NameResolutionError (non-fatal)

reports.ranking_notes: Roughly 14-16 of the top 40 are poor fits for this persona. Over-leveled (6): 6sense 'Software Engineer III' scored 100 and was the #1 inbox row overall (worst possible top pick), plus addepar 'Sr. Backend', airtable 'Compute (8+ YOE)', CDK 'Sr. Software Engineer', Reliable Robotics 'Sr. Simulation' + 'Sr. Flight'. They scored high because the local scorer gives 'software engineer' title 100%, and exclude_titles/seniority_exclude word-boundary-match 'senior' but NOT the 'Sr.' abbreviation, so Sr./III/8+ titles sail through both the gate AND the downrank. Non-US-only remote (~8): MixRank Global, Homeward Latin America, addepar UK, affinipay x2 Czech, Affirm several Canada, Serve Montreal, Enveritas Global — scored 74-84 because any 'remote' row gets loc 100% with no US-work-auth awareness. Wrong role-type/not-full-time (2-3): a 'Software Tester' (QA) and a 'Summer Intern'. Local-scorer verdict: decent first cut but two systematic new-grad blind spots (seniority abbreviations; country-blind remote). The BYO-AI re-rank earned its keep by fixing exactly these; a new grad trusting the raw Score would apply to a Sr. role and a Czech-only remote job.
tracking_gaps: Two overlapping ways to model a phone screen: a phone_screen STATUS and a round kind='phone_screen'; GUI wiring doesn't make clear which is canonical.
add_interview_round takes free-text kind/outcome with no enum validation (fine for power use; a GUI without dropdowns could yield inconsistent values).

No functional gap: Interested->Applied->Interview+rounds->Offer(amount/deadline/notes)->Accepted/Rejected/Ghosted, notes, and timeline all persisted and re-read cleanly via a fresh SQL connection; date_applied + a +7-day follow-up auto-stamp centrally on any path into 'applied'.

verdict.biggest_gap: Seniority-blind and country-blind local scoring: a 'Software Engineer III' scored 100 and topped the inbox, and Canada/Czech/UK-only remote rows got full location credit — so ~14 of the top-40 are false positives a no-AI new grad would see. Compounded by CareerOneStop being unkeyed (the Guide's own #1 local lever) and only ~3% reach of the Austin SWE universe vs the ToS-blocked LinkedIn/Indeed.

bugs: Seniority-abbreviation gate miss (scorer, no traceback): exclude_titles/seniority_exclude word-boundary-match 'senior' but NOT 'Sr.'; Roman numerals ('III') and '8+ YOE' also not downranked. Result: Sr. Software Engineer (CDK), Sr. Backend (addepar), Sr. Simulation/Flight (Reliable Robotics), and Software Engineer III (6sense, scored 100 = #1 row) all survived with title 100%.

Remote scoring ignores country/work-auth: any row containing 'remote' gets loc 100%; Czech-only, UK-only, Canada-only remote rows (affinipay, addepar, several Affirm) scored 75-84 and outranked real Austin jobs despite being unreachable for a US-only new grad.

+Add Companies persists unreachable boards without confirmation: save_companies adds all parsed entries regardless of probe_count; 5 unreachable + 2 junk seeds were all written to companies.json. Evidence: run-time soft errors '[direct] Oracle Austin: fetch error -- NameResolutionError(... not-a-real-ats.example.com)' and same for the junk-line host 'totally%20junk%20line%20no%20url%20here'.
Adzuna rate-limit back-off x2 (~60s each) added ~2 min to a 2m9s run; no hard 429 surfaced (rate/quota event, not a failure).

Keyless sources silently contribute nothing: Jooble, Careerjet, and CareerOneStop are in the daily net but returned 0 with only warning lines — a general user won't notice their local coverage is quietly capped (config/onboarding gap, not a code crash).

setup_friction: Wizard 'Field/industry' and 'Career level' are optional and easy to skip, yet the field answer is load-bearing (turns on the nursing feed + gates out remote-tech boards). Wizard doesn't flag its importance at the skip point (Guide does).

Industry text 'nursing' does NOT resolve to an O*NET SOC code (only 'registered nurse'/'nurse' do -> 29-1141.00), so create_project silently skipped stamping onet_soc_code for a user who types the natural word 'nursing'.
A real nurse cannot supply exact ATS tenant slugs for local hospitals, so the Guide's headline 'add your local employers' step yields 0 verifiable boards without deep ATS knowledge.
CareerOneStop (the Guide's own 'best free source for nurses') is unkeyed out of the box, so the single most nurse-relevant national feed never ran.

seeding.notes: save_companies() adds every parsed line regardless of probe status, so all 14 landed in companies.json tagged 'nursing'. But 0 probed LIVE: 10 were 'direct' (uncountable hospital portals) and 4 AI-guessed ATS slugs were all wrong (St. Lukes Workday stlukesonline:5:External->HTTP422 gone; Primary Health greenhouse 'primaryhealth'->gone; St Lukes Rehab lever 'stlukesrehab'->gone; Kindred workable 'kindredhospital'->gone). Several direct hosts don't resolve (trinity-health.jobs, fullcirclehealth.org, elksrehab.org=DNS fail; HCA=403; Idaho H&W=404). At run time the careers layer scraped these 14 and returned 0 jobs. Seeding contributed nothing usable to the inbox = the flow works as designed (bad guesses fail probe) but is effectively useless for a general nurse who cannot know ATS slugs.

errors: Seeded direct/ATS companies threw soft fetch errors (DNS getaddrinfo fail for trinity-health.jobs/fullcirclehealth.org, ConnectTimeout elksrehab.org, 403 HCA, 404 Idaho H&W, HTTP 422 St Lukes Workday) - all non-fatal
Repeated benign log noise: '[direct] ... link extraction + JSON-LD - verify manually' and BRAVE/jooble/careerjet skip lines re-logged across page-1, page-2, and rescore passes

reports.ranking_notes: ~9 false positives in the top 29 (only 29 rows exist, so top-29 = full inbox). 6x Grand Canyon University 'Adjunct RN Faculty - PRN' scored 65-67 via title 'RN'/nurse 100% + salary 100% + loc 100%, but they are academic clinical-instructor PRN roles spanning Twin Falls/multiple campuses -> off-role AND often out-of-metro. id=7 'RN ... in MONTANA' scored 71 (all-100% components) but the JD body says Montana - Adzuna mislabels it 'Idaho City, Boise County'. id=3 Dialysis RN is Eastern Idaho (~250mi), agency/travel-flavored (HealthTrust). id=26/27 Senior Social Worker-PTSD and id=28 Lead Clinical Laboratory Scientist (USAJobs) matched only on 'clinical' and are not nursing at all; cleared 40 on federal salary + Boise loc padding. Root cause: the local scorer trusts the source's LOCATION LABEL and TITLE-KEYWORD overlap; it never reads the JD body, so a wrong-state or wrong-role posting labeled 'Boise County' clears the on-site filter and scores high. The app's own recommended BYO-AI Fit re-rank cleanly demotes all 9 - i.e. the product tells the user to run exactly the pass that fixes its scorer's blind spot. Local scorer quality: good on salary/remote-gate, weak on label-vs-body location truth and role-vs-title semantics.
tracking_gaps: Interview rounds and offer fields (amount/deadline/notes) are only editable inside the JobDialog (double-click a row), not from the tracker list quick-status dropdown - a discoverability step, not a missing capability.
No blocking gaps found: accepted/ghosted statuses, interview_rounds, status_history (transitions + note-only), and offer_* columns all persisted and are all wired to GUI buttons.

verdict.biggest_gap: The entire local inbox rode on Adzuna (76%) + USAJobs (24%), both keyed for this test; a real nurse with no keys would get ~7 federal-only jobs. The nurse-specific RNJobSite feed returned 244 postings but 0 survived the Boise location gate (national/unlocalized), the eng-shaped starter registry has zero nursing employers, seeding produced 0 live boards (needs ATS slugs no nurse knows), and CareerOneStop - the Guide's own 'best free source for nurses' - is unkeyed. The single biggest fix: make CareerOneStop work out of the box (or bundle a keyless equivalent) so the free national all-employer government feed fills the gap without the user guessing hospital ATS slugs.

bugs: _attach_onet_soc gap (workspace.py:345): industry text 'nursing' does not resolve to a SOC code (only 'registered nurse'/'nurse' do -> 29-1141.00), so a project created with the natural field word 'nursing' silently gets no onet_soc_code/onet_soc_title. Cosmetic here (nursing gating keys on text tokens) but a real routing-stability gap. No traceback (silent no-op by design in _attach_onet_soc's try/except).

Location gate trusts the source's location LABEL not the JD body: Adzuna labels many out-of-metro/out-of-state RN postings as 'Idaho City, Boise County', so id=7 (Montana), id=3 (Eastern Idaho ~250mi), and 6 GCU faculty (Twin Falls ~130mi) all pass the on-site Boise filter with loc 100% and score 65-76. Not a crash - a relevance defect. ~3 out-of-metro + ~6 off-role rows leaked into a 29-row inbox.

save_companies adds companies regardless of probe result (company_registry.py:189): the '+ Add Companies' Add button appends every parsed line even when Validate showed 'unreachable'/'gone', so 14/14 seeded (4 dead ATS guesses included) landed in companies.json. Not incorrect by contract (probe is advisory) but means a user's dead-slug guesses persist and get re-scraped (throwing soft errors) every daily run.

Log noise: page-1, page-2, and rescore passes each re-emit the same '[direct] ... verify manually' and BRAVE/jooble/careerjet skip lines, so a 29-job run produced dozens of duplicate skip/verify lines - harmless but hides real signal for a non-technical user reading the console.

setup_friction: Starter registry ships with only health_informatics + controls_engineering — ZERO education companies; wizard never says so, and a teacher who skips the optional Field box + Build-My-List step gets a company layer that adds nothing.

resolve_soc('education') and resolve_soc('education (K-12)') both return None, so create_project attached no O*NET-SOC code; only 'math teacher' resolves and it maps to POSTSECONDARY (wrong level for 7-12). Genre routing still works, but the SOC that would sharpen title scoring is absent.
Nothing warns that public school districts run Frontline (no scraper) or NEOGOV/governmentjobs (ToS-blocked) — a teacher's most obvious targets are structurally unaddable.
Career-level combobox (Entry/Mid/Senior/Manager-Exec) has no notion of teacher->coach->curriculum ladder; only the free-text 'Anything else' box can express it.

preferences.json/preferences.md are not scaffolded by create_project (only config.json + experience.md stub); a real GUI user gets them via the wizard's apply(), but a programmatic creator must write them by hand.

seeding.notes: 16 'Name | ATS-URL' lines pushed through the exact +Add Companies pipeline (parse_line -> probe_count -> save_companies). All 16 parsed and all 16 SAVED (GUI adds every detected entry; probe is advisory). Probe results: 4 live-with-jobs (Newsela 17, Guild 11, OSU/Workday 1041, Khan 22), 1 live-but-zero (Battelle SmartRecruiters), 11 unreachable (dead slug guesses: KIPP/IDEA/Uncommon/DemocracyPrep/SuccessAcademy/Amplify/IXL/CurriculumAssociates/VarsityTutors/Paper/TeachForAmerica). Deliberately leaned charter/ed-tech/nonprofit/higher-ed because Columbus public districts run Frontline/NEOGOV (unscrapeable/ToS-blocked). After save, get_registry(industry='education') returns 16 (shipped registry had 0) — registry generalized entirely from the seed. But yield into inbox was tiny: only OSU actually fed the inbox (13 careers rows, all OSU non-K12 noise); the teacher-adjacent boards (Newsela/Khan/Guild) contributed 0 gated rows this run.

errors: [careeronestop] Skipping — credentials missing (KNOWN GAP, and the Guide itself calls CareerOneStop the #1 free source for teachers)
[jooble] key unset — skipped (many warning lines, harmless self-skip)
[careerjet] affid unset — skipped
[discover] BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY unset — company discovery skipped, registry-only
11 seeded Greenhouse/Lever boards logged 'gone — skipping' (same dead slugs the probe flagged; non-fatal)

reports.ranking_notes: ~9-11 false positives in the top 40, and the WORST are ranked HIGHEST. #1-#3 by on-device Score (all 87) are OSU 'Lead Teacher 2/3' — infant/toddler CHILDCARE lab-school roles, not secondary math. score_notes shows 'title 100% | loc 67% | size +4': the generic keyword 'teacher' matched 100%, OSU-Columbus gave loc, and the 1041-job board gave a size bonus. The scorer has NO grade-band awareness — cannot distinguish daycare 'Lead Teacher' from a 7-12 math teacher. Also FP: OSU Assistant Teacher x3 (61); OSU veterinary/livestock/dentistry/poultry 'Program Coordinator/Instructor' (40-44, correctly hit title-miss -18 and sank to floor but still occupy slots); 3 out-of-state Adzuna mis-stamps (Paris SSD/TN, Maury County/TN, Randolph County/NC-GA) only 'local' because Adzuna copies the query location onto every posting. Root causes: broad 'teacher' keyword with no grade-band negative; OSU's giant Workday board injecting non-K12 noise; Adzuna location stamping. Local scorer is EXCELLENT on location (dropped 976 wrong-loc), WEAK on role/grade-band nuance — exactly the split the app advertises (Score=overlap, Fit=nuance). The BYO-AI re-rank demoted every OSU/childcare/out-of-state row and floated the true math/coach seats the raw Score buried at 64-67.
tracking_gaps: No blocking gaps — applied, interview, offer, accepted, rejected, ghosted all exist and persisted; interview rounds (phone_screen + onsite) and per-stage notes work through the service layer.
Minor: both a distinct 'phone_screen' STATUS and a 'phone_screen' round KIND exist — a GUI user could conflate them.
offer_amount/offer_deadline/offer_notes are free-text (acceptable). date_applied auto-stamped and +7d follow-up auto-armed on the applied transition (centralized side-effect).

verdict.biggest_gap: CareerOneStop is unkeyed (the app's own Guide calls it the #1 free source for teachers, yet it silently self-skips) AND public-district portals on Frontline/NEOGOV are unreachable — so the two places K-12 district jobs actually live are both dark. For an on-site teacher, Adzuna does ~100% of the useful work, which makes the missing CareerOneStop key the whole ballgame.

bugs: No crashes/tracebacks — daily_run exited 0; create_project, the seeding pipeline, and the full lifecycle all succeeded first try.

Observation (not a bug): daily_run.py --project X calls workspace.set_active(X) at line 218, so running the persona's update flipped on-disk projects.json 'active' from gu-nurse-boise to gu-teacher-columbus — a general user running one project's update silently changes which project the GUI opens to next.

Data-quality (upstream Adzuna, worth surfacing): Adzuna stamps the query location onto every posting; 3/49 inbox rows are out-of-state employers shown as 'Columbus, Franklin County'. A 'verify location against description' pass would catch these.

Scorer limitation (known/acceptable): generic 'teacher' keyword produces title-100% bleed that floats daycare 'Lead Teacher' roles to the very top; no grade-band awareness. This is exactly what the BYO-AI channel exists to fix, and it did.

setup_friction: industry 'management consulting' did NOT resolve to an O*NET-SOC code in workspace._attach_onet_soc (no onet_soc_code written), though it resolves fine in industry_profile for routing/synonyms/title-terms
the wizard's optional field/industry box shows no consulting example (only health/nursing/finance/controls), so a consulting user might leave it blank and mis-route
starter registry ships only health_informatics + controls_engineering -> a consulting user's careers path is empty on day one until seeding, which then mostly fails (see seeding notes)
resume step doesn't state the resume is mainly used once an AI key is set for tailoring; ranking leans on preferences.md

seeding.notes: Pushed 17 'Name | URL' consulting-firm lines through the real +Add-Companies pipeline (ats_detect.parse_line -> probe_count -> company_registry.save_companies) tagged management_consulting. save_companies ADDED all 17 (0 skipped) because the dialog does NOT gate on the probe — probe is advisory only. But only 1 probed LIVE with jobs (Point B, lever:pointb, 9 open); 14 Greenhouse/Lever slug guesses were UNREACHABLE; 2 (Deloitte, McKinsey) parsed as 'direct' (JS/JSON-LD, uncountable). On the daily run all 14 unreachable logged 'gone -> skipping' and the 2 direct fell back to best-effort JSON-LD. So the ask-your-AI seeding flow added ~0 usable local coverage for consulting: major firms run enterprise ATSes (Workday/Avature/custom) behind CSRF/JS, and the AI cannot reliably guess public-board slugs. Probe pipeline itself verified healthy (SpaceX greenhouse control returned 1829). companies.json left for janitor to restore.

errors: careeronestop skipped — credentials missing (known unkeyed gap; biggest local miss)
jooble/careerjet/brave skipped — no keys
higheredjobs + rnjobsite correctly inert for a non-education/non-nursing field
14 seeded ATS companies logged 'gone -> skipping'; Deloitte/McKinsey 'direct' JSON-LD verify-manually
CareersClient returned 0 despite Point B being live with 9 open — likely keyword/location filtering after scrape, not a confirmed bug
reach 'cannot certify' — no cross-source overlap (no SerpApi key)

reports.ranking_notes: Local scorer is strong on locality+field but weak on ROLE disambiguation. ~19 of the top 40 (~48%) are false positives/weak fits, driven almost entirely by the literal title token 'Engagement Manager': ~12 Amazon 'AWS ProServe / Community Engagement Manager' rows (cloud-implementation delivery, not strategy consulting — they took the top 3 slots at scores 91-93 purely on title+high salary), plus Google gTech Ads EM (#6, also NY/Atlanta primary = location noise), Edwards Lifesciences 'Provider Education & Engagement' (a medtech sales role, #21), Capital One PM 'Engagement Manager' x2 (#24/#34), Legora customer-success EM x2 (#30/#31), and Workday EM AI-Practice (#35, SaaS delivery). Guidehouse Strategy Sr Consultant (#39) is real consulting but FEMA/Public-Trust clearance ('ability to obtain') — borderline vs her clearance deal-breaker, and the preferences.json dealbreaker gate did NOT catch it (matches 'active clearance', not 'ability to obtain'). The remaining ~21 are genuine consulting/strategy fits. This is exactly the gap the BYO-AI Fit round-trip is meant to close and is the highest-value AI use for this persona. per-company cap is per-run so it didn't trim the 12 distinct AWS postings.
tracking_gaps: follow_up_date (+7d, armed on entering 'applied') is NOT cleared when an app reaches a terminal state — all 5 rows including accepted/rejected/ghosted still show fu=2026-07-08; count_followups_due filters to applied/phone_screen/interview so it won't nag, but the stale date lingers on the row (DB hygiene/cosmetic)
offer_amount is free-text only (string) — fine for tracking, no structured comp for analytics
no true blocker: all statuses (incl. S29 accepted + ghosted), interview rounds, notes, and offer terms are reachable from the module API the GUI buttons call

verdict.biggest_gap: The local scorer cannot disambiguate the 'engagement manager' title family (strategy/management-consulting EMs vs AWS cloud-delivery / customer-success / internal-PM EMs), so ~half the top 40 are false positives that bury true strategy roles below AWS listings; combined with CareerOneStop unkeyed, the run is ~95% single-source (Adzuna).

bugs: SEEDING/UX (not a crash): scrape.company_registry.save_companies (called by AddCompaniesDialog._add in gui.py) adds EVERY parsed line regardless of probe result — probe is advisory only. Seeding 17 consulting firms wrote all 17 into companies.json though only 1 (Point B) probes live; 16 dead entries silently persist and no-op ('gone -> skipping') on every daily run. No traceback (by design), but for consulting the ask-your-AI flow adds ~0 usable coverage and the user is never told which entries failed.

MINOR: workspace._attach_onet_soc does not resolve industry 'management consulting' to an O*NET-SOC code (no onet_soc_code written to config.json), while industry_profile.resolve DOES handle the 'consulting' token — so SOC-code tagging silently no-ops for consulting though routing/synonyms work. No traceback.

MINOR: preferences.json dealbreaker gate ('active clearance','ts/sci','security clearance') does not catch a posting whose text says 'Clearance Required: Ability to Obtain Public Trust' (Guidehouse #39 surfaced in top 40). Substring gate misses the 'ability to obtain' phrasing. No traceback.

OBSERVATION (unconfirmed): CareersClient returned 0 in the daily run despite the one live seed (Point B, lever:pointb, 9 open jobs) being present in the management-consulting registry and NOT logged as 'gone' — likely post-scrape keyword/location filtering, but the single scrapeable seed produced nothing. Not reproduced as a defect.

setup_friction: Wizard never warns that a two-word field/industry ('warehouse logistics') behaves differently for the company registry than a one-word one - it silently matches 0 companies (bug B1), so the careers path is starved even after seeding
Location field is free-text city with no explicit radius control; the '20 mi' intent is implicit (it worked, but the user cannot set a radius)

No warehouse/logistics industry_profile exists: resolve_soc returns None for warehouse operations/lead/distribution/forklift, so query_synonyms is empty and keyword-broadening is a no-op for this field
CareerOneStop (the DOL feed the Guide names as the #2 key and the best free source for blue-collar on-site work) is unkeyed, so a general user's biggest local equalizer is missing

seeding.notes: 16 real Memphis logistics/distribution employers + 1 deliberate junk line, pushed through the exact GUI '+ Add Companies' pipeline (ats_detect.parse_line -> probe_count -> company_registry.save_companies). ALL 17 were added to companies.json - the Add step does NOT gate on the Validate probe (bug B2), so the junk and 9 UNREACHABLE boards were saved anyway. Only 2 probed live (Flexport 129, Technicolor 0); 6 were 'direct' (uncountable best-effort: FedEx/XPO/GXO/Ryder/DHL/Kuehne+Nagel); 9 unreachable (Workday CSRF 422 for AutoZone/IntlPaper, 404 wrong-site-slug for Nike, dead GH slug for junk). The marquee Memphis employers a warehouse worker most wants (FedEx/Nike/AutoZone/Int'l Paper) are on CSRF-protected Workday portals the scraper cannot pull. Also, because config.industry='warehouse logistics' (two words), bug B1 meant NONE of these 17 were even scraped on the run.

reports.ranking_notes: Local scorer quality is good for locality (100% in-area after the location hard-gate dropped 193 out-of-area) but poor at DOMAIN discrimination: it rewards title tokens (lead/shift/supervisor/warehouse) without understanding the field. ~12 false positives in the top ~40 scored 60-72: Industrial Maintenance Tech x3 (Bosch), Weld Production Leader (AAON), Electrical/Mechanical Lead x2 (Jabil), Quality Technician (Bosch), Warehouse Janitorial Cleaner (ABM), Warehouse Office Support (Home Depot), Starbucks store shift supervisor, Valet Shift Lead at Graceland (Towne Park), and a brewery cellar/shift person (Wiseacre). All matched on 'shift'/'lead'/'warehouse' tokens. The BYO-AI re-rank cleanly demoted every one of them to fit 12-30 while promoting real DC-lead/supervisor/coordinator roles to 80-95 - the textbook 'high Score, low Fit' the Guide describes. Score band was compressed (top 82, most 60-69, none >=90).
tracking_gaps: B4 data-loss trap: tracker.db.update_job silently ignores unknown field names - update_job(offer_salary='53669') was dropped with no error/warning; the real column is offer_amount. A user or their AI guessing a field name loses data silently

B3: apply_rerank_scores (file-import/'Load AI results' route) writes the Fit column but does NOT stamp rank/rec_batch, so top_picks() stays empty for that route - only the clipboard 'Paste AI ranking' route (score_inbox_from_reply) populates the Top Picks tab. File-round-trip users get a Fit-sortable inbox but an empty Top Picks tab
No functional gap otherwise: all 9 statuses reachable incl. S29 accepted+ghosted; interview rounds, status notes, and the full timeline all persisted and re-read correctly

verdict.biggest_gap: Local warehouse results ride ~100% on the single keyed Adzuna aggregator: the careers/registry path added 0 (bug B1 + marquee employers FedEx/Nike/AutoZone/Int'l Paper being on CSRF-protected Workday portals the scraper cannot pull), the keyless sources (CareerOneStop/Jooble/Careerjet) added 0, and the app is ToS-blocked from Indeed - the dominant board for blue-collar warehouse work. Fix B1 + key CareerOneStop and this persona is well-served; until then it is one-source-fragile.

bugs: B1 (real bug): a multi-word industry value never matches the company registry. get_registry normalizes the industry key spaces->underscores ('warehouse logistics'->'warehouse_logistics') but company tags keep the space, and _industry_tag_match uses plain substring containment which the underscore breaks both ways. Measured: industry_company_count('warehouse logistics')==0 while ('logistics')==17 and ('warehouse')==17, all 17 seeded cos tagged ['warehouse logistics']. Daily run logged 'only 0 registry companies match' and careers added 0 despite fresh seeding. Fix in scrape/company_registry.py _industry_tag_match.

B2 (design gap): AddCompaniesDialog._add / company_registry.save_companies write every parsed line to companies.json regardless of the Validate probe result - junk ('Zip Code Nonsense LLC') and all 9 UNREACHABLE Workday/greenhouse boards were saved. Validate is purely informational; no 'add only validated' option. gui.py ~L2428.

B3 (minor): tracker.service.apply_rerank_scores writes fit but not rank/rec_batch, so tracker.service.top_picks() returns empty for the file-import route; only score_inbox_from_reply (clipboard paste) populates Top Picks.

B4 (data-loss trap): tracker.db.update_job silently ignores unknown field names (only keys in _EXTRA_COLUMNS are written). update_job(offer_salary=...) dropped the value with no error; real column is offer_amount.

Non-bug findings: no warehouse/logistics industry_profile (resolve_soc returns None for warehouse operations/lead/distribution/forklift; empty query_synonyms; broaden_keywords no-op); gate_tech_sources does NOT drop remote-tech boards (remoteok/remotive/jobicy/himalayas/hn/weworkremotely/workingnomads) for a warehouse field, wasting calls though not producing wrong results; CareerOneStop/Jooble/Careerjet/Brave keys unset (known gaps); no 429/quota events observed.

setup_friction: Location step has no explicit 'remote-only' toggle; a remote user types 'Remote' as their city + ticks the checkbox, which then makes the geocoded aggregators (Adzuna/USAJobs) return 0.
Industry/field box silently drives source routing, query-synonyms, and the company-tag filter, but the wizard doesn't explain the stakes; leaving it blank auto-assigns an O*NET occupation title.

Wizard-derived industry ('Advertising and Promotions Managers') does NOT tag-match seeded companies (tagged 'digital_marketing'), so a normal wizard finish would seed 13 employers the daily run then never scrapes. I set industry='digital marketing' manually to fix this.
Typing industry='digital marketing' triggers health-informatics synonym pollution in query broadening; I set broaden_keywords=false to work around it.
Enabled Jooble/Careerjet in config but no keys present -> silent skip. CareerOneStop (Guide's #2 key) unkeyed, so the DOL/NLx feed was absent.

seeding.notes: Pushed 15 AI-suggested 'Name | ATS URL' lines through the real GUI pipeline (ats_detect.parse_line -> probe_count -> company_registry.save_companies). All 15 parsed; 6 probed LIVE (GitLab 141, Remote.com 294, Vercel 65, Webflow 24, Toptal 16, Doximity 11), 3 parsed as 'direct'/uncountable (HubSpot/Buffer/Grammarly - I gave marketing-landing-page URLs not ATS slugs), 6 probed unreachable 404 (Zapier/Automattic/Sourcegraph/Deel/Clipboard Health/1Password - all off those exact Greenhouse/Lever slugs now). save_companies adds every parsed entry regardless of probe (probe is advisory in the GUI), so 13 landed; 2 skipped as already-present dups (Doximity was in the shipped health registry). GitLab/Vercel/Webflow seeds produced this run's top 4 inbox rows. 'rejected'=skipped-as-dup count; the 6 unreachable/3 direct were still saved.

reports.ranking_notes: "Only 8 rows inboxed, so the 'top 40' IS the whole inbox. 4 of 8 are false positives (50% tail FP rate): GitLab-EMEA (region-locked remote), BlueBox ($18k UK-hours contract - see bug 5), StubGroup (non-US remote + $48-60k), BrowserStack (Product Design, not marketing). They scored 41-66 because the local scorer over-weights the token 'Manager' and the word 'remote' and under-weights (a) region qualifiers inside a remote string (EMEA/LatAm-only) and (b) sub-floor comp that only appears in the description, not salary_text. The two GitLab US roles (my seeds) correctly topped at 79/66, so the scorer didn't miss the winner. Local-scorer quality: good at the head, noisy in the tail."
tracking_gaps: No blocking gaps. Full status set (interested->applied->phone_screen->interview->offer->accepted/rejected/withdrawn/ghosted), interview_rounds, per-stage notes, and offer fields (amount/deadline/notes) are all reachable via the same functions the GUI buttons call and all persisted (verified by independent DB re-read: 1 accepted/1 interview/1 applied/1 rejected/1 ghosted, 3 interview rounds, 14 status_history rows).
Minor: entering 'applied' auto-stamps date_applied and arms a +7-day follow-up on EVERY path - good, but a user might not expect the auto follow-up date.
Minor: phone_screen is a valid status but I jumped straight to interview; both work.

verdict.biggest_gap: Remote-only search is unproductive on the keyed aggregators: Adzuna (the Guide's #1 unlock) and USAJobs both returned 0 for location='Remote', so a remote knowledge-worker leans almost entirely on WeWorkRemotely/RemoteOK + self-seeded career pages -> only 8 jobs in one run, half the tail noise, vs 100+ in one LinkedIn scroll for 'remote marketing manager'.

bugs: Adzuna returns 0 for location='Remote' (both passes: '[AdzunaClient] 0 results'). USAJobs also 0. Not a crash - a coverage gap that guts the value prop for remote knowledge-workers, and it's precisely the source the Guide calls the #1 'biggest unlock'.

Industry-synonym pollution: industry_profile.resolve('digital marketing') has no marketing profile and falls back to health-informatics defaults, so broad_query_keywords(..., 'digital marketing') injects ['clinical informatics','healthcare analytics','health data','electronic health record','epic'] into a marketing query. A user who types 'digital marketing' gets healthcare noise. Verified via search.keyword_strategy.broad_query_keywords.

industry_profile.resolve_soc('demand generation manager') mis-resolves to '11-3051.06 Hydroelectric Production Managers'. Harmless here (didn't let it set industry) but would mislead auto-detect. 'growth marketing manager' resolves to None; 'marketing manager' -> 'Advertising and Promotions Managers'.

Silent careers-path zeroing: the wizard derives industry='Advertising and Promotions Managers' for a marketing user, but seeded companies get tagged 'digital_marketing'. industry_company_count('Advertising and Promotions Managers')==0 vs ==13 for 'digital marketing'; _industry_tag_match('digital marketing','digital_marketing') is even False (only get_registry's containment path saves it). Net: a user finishing the wizard normally would seed 13 employers the daily run then never scrapes. Most impactful trap; I worked around it by setting industry='digital marketing'.

Sub-floor salary in the description slips the $90k hard-gate: BlueBox's real comp 'US$1,500 per Month' (~$18k) but its salary_text field is '$18,000+', which salary_from_text() parses to (None,None) -> 'unknown salary => kept'. The gate never reads the description, so a clearly sub-$90k role passed a $90k floor and scored 65 (inbox top-4).

setup_friction: Field/industry box is free text with no validation and no match against the registry's actual tags — the direct cause of BUG 1 (two-word field label never matches seeded companies).
Senior level must be set via the Career-level combobox, not the role text; easy to miss.
Nothing warns the user that a two-word field label ('mechanical engineering') will silently match 0 registry/seeded companies.
create_project scaffolds config.json/experience.md/output only; preferences.json/md must be written separately (the wizard's apply() does this, but a programmatic caller must replicate the shape).

seeding.notes: 15 lines parsed to CompanyEntry via ats_detect.parse_line; probe_count validated; save_companies added 8 (Stoke Space, Radiant, Divergent, Zipline, Relativity, Formic, Fictiv, Seurat) + 1 direct (Boeing) submitted but Boeing counted in the 8-vs-9: save_companies returned 8 added, 1 skipped (already present). 6 rejected on 404 probe (Blue Origin, Glowforge, Carbon Robotics, Kymeta, McKinstry-Workday, PACCAR-Workday). KEY FRICTION: a chat-only AI reliably produces company NAMES but frequently WRONG ATS slugs — first-pass guesses for well-known Seattle employers 404'd (network was fine; Divergent 200'd same run). Had to fall back to slugs I could verify (several already in starter registry). The pipeline rejected bad slugs gracefully (flow working as designed) but yield is low without web browsing. CRITICAL: all 8 added tagged industries=['mechanical engineering'] but get_registry(industry='mechanical engineering') returns 0 due to BUG 1 (space-vs-underscore) — so they will NEVER be searched by this project's careers path. companies.json left for janitor to restore; not restored by me.

errors: NOTE: 0 registry companies match industry 'mechanical engineering' -> careers path added 0 jobs (BUG 1 symptom)

jooble skipped: JOOBLE_API_KEY unset
careerjet skipped: CAREERJET_AFFID unset

reports.ranking_notes: Top 40 = all 32 inbox rows (fit=-1 on all, no auto-AI used; I was the BYO-AI re-ranker). 8 false positives, all explained by score_notes showing title-match dominates and skills is a weak counterweight: (1) Railway 73 HN 'Infra Eng...Brand Designer...Product Eng' -> title 100%/skills 12%, software infra, HIGHEST-scored and a total miss; (2) Hospitable 'Staff UI/UX Product Designer' 72 -> 'product designer'~='product design engineer', title 100%/skills 25%; (3) jerry.ai 'Staff Product Designer' 68 same; (4) DigitalOcean 'Product Security Engineer, Secure Design' 67 -> 'design' token, title 100%/skills 0%; (5) BrowserStack 'Manager Product Design' 62 software; (6) Qualtrics 'Staff PM Product Design & Engineering' 50; (7) Solace Health Mobile Engineer 43 software; (8) Kosmos Mechanical Engineer 64 = REAL ME role but Butte MT mislabeled 'Seattle' by Adzuna (locality FP, source error not app). Local scorer quality: locality gate is excellent (25/32 truly in-area after dropping 280) and salary/dealbreaker gates work; the weakness is title-token over-weighting for a keyword containing 'design' pulling in software/UX. The BYO-AI re-rank cleanly demotes all 8.
tracking_gaps: No blocking gaps — all S29 statuses (accepted, ghosted) and interview rounds work from the tracker.service layer that GUI buttons call.
date_applied + a +7-day follow-up are auto-stamped centrally on the applied transition (db.update_job), so every entry path arms the same follow-up engine (verified in code).
Minor: status_timeline folds status into old_status/new_status columns in status_history; a reader must join those (not user-facing).
DB paths resolve from workspace.active_slug(), so any programmatic tracker call must set_active or pin_active the project first — a subtle footgun for scripted use, invisible in the GUI.

verdict.biggest_gap: Careers/company-registry path yields 0 for this persona's field due to the industry-tag space-vs-underscore bug (BUG 1), so his 8 verified employers are never searched; combined with unkeyed CareerOneStop/Jooble/Careerjet this makes the run effectively Adzuna-only for local jobs.

bugs: BUG 1 (HIGH, persona-blocking, silent wrong-result, no traceback): scrape/company_registry.py _industry_tag_match (~L234) does not normalize the company tag while get_registry (~L300) normalizes the industry key (spaces->underscores). So a company tagged ['mechanical engineering'] never matches industry 'mechanical engineering': _industry_tag_match('mechanical_engineering','mechanical engineering')==False, and get_registry(industry='mechanical engineering')==0 even after 8 companies were added tagged that way. Any general user with a 2+word field label ('mechanical engineering','product design','supply chain','data science') has their '+ Add Companies' additions silently invisible and their daily careers path contributes 0; daily_run logs 'only 0 registry companies match industry'. Single-word fields work; starter tags are single-token (controls_engineering) so single-project eng users never hit it. Fix: normalize the tag inside _industry_tag_match (t.replace(' ','_')). Verified live this run: careers=0 for this persona.

BUG 2 (LOW, upstream data quality, not the app's fault): Adzuna returned location 'Seattle, King County' for a Butte, MT role (Kosmos 'Mechanical Engineer', score 64); it passed the location hard-gate. The description contradicts the location field. A description-vs-location sanity check would catch such source mislabels.

setup_friction: Industry is optional free text with no picker/validation — typing "data analytics" (the natural phrasing) triggers a space-vs-underscore normalization bug that silently excludes every company the user later adds via + Add Companies from their own careers searches.

AI-assisted seeding could only reliably resolve Greenhouse slugs (guessable from the board URL); the 9 biggest local employers Nicole most wants (Banner Health, Phoenix Children's, HonorHealth, APS, SRP + 2 Lever cos) came back 'unreachable' because a public careers URL doesn't reveal the correct Workday tenant slug — no recovery path for a non-technical user.
CareerOneStop unkeyed = the single biggest missing local unlock for this field; the local hospital/utility/university/state analyst jobs never arrive, forcing the user back to LinkedIn.
Resume auto-structuring leaves the name/contact block un-sectioned (bare above the first promoted heading) and doesn't recognize 'DATA ANALYTICS BOOTCAMP' as a heading — cosmetic, no data lost.

seeding.notes: All 15 AI-produced 'Name | URL' lines parsed to CompanyEntry and were added by save_companies (probe is advisory-only in the + Add Companies dialog, so nothing is rejected at save time). Probe outcomes: 2 LIVE (Axon 522, Carvana 2103 — both Greenhouse, both Phoenix-HQ analyst employers), 4 direct/uncountable (ASU, Grand Canyon Univ, OpenDoor, Choice Hotels), 9 unreachable (Western Alliance, Progressive Leasing, Banner Health, Phoenix Children's, HonorHealth, APS, SRP via wrong/CSRF Workday slugs; Nextiva, Trainual via 404 Lever slugs). CRITICAL: all 15 were tagged industry 'data analytics' (space form the wizard stores) but get_registry normalizes the key to 'data_analytics' and matches against the un-normalized tag, so ALL 15 are silently excluded from the persona's own careers scrape — industry_company_count('data analytics') stayed at 8 after adding 15.

errors: CareerOneStop skipped — no key (known gap)
Jooble/Careerjet/Brave-discovery skipped — no keys (clean self-skip)
higheredjobs inert (no education categories for data-analytics); rnjobsite inert (not nursing)
Reach badge: cannot certify — no SerpApi key, disjoint source families, ~28% Good-Turing completeness
preflight NOTE: only 8 registry companies match industry 'data analytics' — careers path adds few/no jobs (downstream effect of the industry-tag bug)

reports.ranking_notes: Of the top 40 by local score, roughly 14-16 are poor fits for a no-experience career-changer and they cluster at the very top. Root cause from score_notes: any title containing 'analyst'/'data' gets title 100% regardless of seniority, any disclosed salary above the $55k floor gets salary 100%, Phoenix gets loc 67%, plus freshness. The entry-level config (seniority_target=entry, years_cap=3) feeds the AI rubric but barely touches the LOCAL keyword scorer. False positives: #1 Senior Data Analyst (System One, contract), #3/#11 Sr Analyst (Maximus), #4 Senior Data Solutions (Highmark), #5 Senior Preconstruction (Ryan), #6 Senior Analyst Data Governance (Amex), #20 Senior Data Analyst (Stride), #40 Principal BI Analyst (Cytel); plus role-mislabels #15 Ops Research/Data Scientist (GovCIO), #23/#38/#39 Mayo 'Data Science Analyst' (AI/ML/deep-learning); plus clearance/contract-heavy #9 (banking regulatory, $60-70/hr, 11 yrs), #19 Network Security (Noblis). NONE are location false positives (locality was clean 67 in-area / 10 remote / 0 wrong). Local scorer quality: strong on location and title-keyword recall, weak on SENIORITY/LEVEL discrimination — the single most impactful scoring fix for this persona.
tracking_gaps: add_status_note records the note as a same-status -> same-status row in status_history (e.g. 'accepted -> accepted'), which reads oddly as a no-op status change in a timeline view — functionally correct, cosmetically confusing.

No functional gaps: all required statuses (interested/applied/phone_screen/interview/offer/accepted/rejected/withdrawn/ghosted) exist and persisted; interview rounds auto-increment round_no; offer_amount/deadline/notes persist; every step maps to a GUI button.

verdict.biggest_gap: CareerOneStop is unkeyed and AI-assisted + Add Companies can't resolve the biggest local employers' Workday/Lever slugs, so the marquee Phoenix employers (Banner Health, Dignity, city/county, ASU) never reach the inbox — forcing the user back to LinkedIn for exactly the jobs a career-changer most wants.

bugs: INDUSTRY-TAG SPACE/UNDERSCORE MISMATCH (silent data-exclusion, no traceback): scrape/company_registry.py get_registry normalizes the industry key via industry.lower().replace(' ','_') -> 'data_analytics' but _industry_tag_match compares it to the un-normalized company tag 'data analytics' (as the wizard stores industry and AddCompaniesDialog passes it). Neither contains the other, so companies a general user adds with their own multi-word wizard industry are excluded from their own careers searches/daily run. Repro: py -3.12 -c "from scrape.company_registry import _industry_tag_match; print(_industry_tag_match('data_analytics','data analytics'))" -> False. Effect: 15 seeded companies added but industry_company_count('data analytics') stayed 8. (Existing spawned task task_f39d0974 already covers this; not re-filed.)

DESIGN GAP (not a crash): entry-level config (seniority_target=entry, years_cap=3) does not down-rank Senior/Sr/Lead/Principal TITLES in the local match scorer — such roles get identical title 100% to a plain Data Analyst and dominate the top of a career-changer's inbox (8+ of top-40).

MINOR: ui/setup_wizard.py structure_resume_text Path A promotes recognized ALL-CAPS headings but leaves the leading name/email/phone contact block un-wrapped (no ## CONTACT) and doesn't recognize 'DATA ANALYTICS BOOTCAMP' as a heading; no data lost but the parser won't cleanly associate contact info.
MINOR: tracker add_status_note is stored as a same-status self-transition in status_history, appearing as a no-op status change in a timeline view.

result.notes: Registry snapshot restored (companies.baseline.json → companies.json, 62,269 bytes). File sizes verified matching. One daily_run.py process (PID 31388) terminated.

companies.json restored from baseline snapshot (62269 bytes, verified match). One lingering daily_run.py process found (PID 2056) but termination blocked by permission classifier — requires user-authorized cleanup via elevated PS or task manager.
Cleanup complete: companies.json restored from baseline, file sizes match, no leftover daily_run.py processes found.
